117220.explanation: For a public park, this can be a nice solution, but in a sports hall we prefer a commercial party.

117222.explanation: We welcome entrepreneurship as long as it is not burdensome to the environment.

117223.explanation: There are provincial and national subsidies to make your house more sustainable which can be applied for through the municipality.

117224.explanation: If it creates jobs, if we have space for it, and it fits in a plan, we see no reason to oppose it.

117226.explanation: Housing on private grounds has the advantage of fewer travel movements to pick up or drop off employees. This way houses remain available for residents.

117231.explanation: Currently, collecting the first cubic meter of bulky waste is free.

117238.explanation: The municipality of Kapelle supports Music School Zeeland, which the VVD endorses. This ensures music lessons are affordable for many families.

117239.explanation: Not unlimited financial contributions but thinking along about how a club can organize itself better.

117242.explanation: Temporary camera surveillance can work at certain locations.

117251.explanation: In Kapelle, the income limit for assistance is set at 120% of the social assistance standard.

117254.explanation: This is already the case in Kapelle.

117255.explanation: A caregiver should be neutral according to the VVD. Regardless of background, they provide the necessary care. The care recipient cannot have expectations in these times of staff shortages.

117256.explanation: With equal quality, price is decisive.

117257.explanation: Should only be indexed with inflation.

117272.explanation: A connection via Vierwegen to Wemeldinge is a good way to relieve the center of Kapelle from car traffic.

117274.explanation: Due to the pick-up and drop-off of the vans, it is desirable that Special Schools no longer remain in residential neighborhoods.

117275.explanation: We would like to keep a small recycling center for residents, provided it meets a need.

117219.explanation: Residents should have a say in important decisions. We also want more input from residents in construction projects, aiming to prevent objections.

117220.explanation: We support room for initiative and ownership from society.

117222.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be given space, within reasonable and clearly known limits in advance.

117223.explanation: We are not in favor of municipal subsidies for energy projects.

117224.explanation: The municipality should not automatically reject distribution centers, but initiatives must be critically assessed to prevent over-industrialization.

117225.explanation: There is no suitable location for an asylum center within Kapelle municipality; additionally, the costs for emergency shelter are exorbitantly high. The municipality should also advocate this provincially and nationally.

117226.explanation: Housing for labor migrants must not come at the expense of housing for our own residents.

117230.explanation: Gemeentebelang supports fireworks clubs where residents organize fireworks shows under supervision. These may receive subsidies similar to Orange associations. However, organizing fireworks shows is not a core task of the municipality.

117231.explanation: Nothing is free; the costs always fall on residents. We make strict choices regarding usefulness and necessity.

117234.explanation: Farmers must be given space for development within pre-agreed frameworks. Especially if this helps our youth get housing faster.

117236.explanation: There are schemes such as Stichting Leergeld, the Youth Sports Fund, and the Adult Fund for Sports & Culture to support sports participation. Social assistance is the social minimum and intended as a temporary provision; we want to maintain this principle.

117238.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities to take music lessons; additional municipal subsidy is not needed. The municipality cannot facilitate all types of musical instruments. Supply and demand remain important in subsidy allocation.

117239.explanation: Sports clubs operate with membership fees, with the municipality facilitating facilities. If income is too low, member recruitment is up to the club. If that fails, the demand is clearly too limited. The municipality cannot enable all sports.

117241.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety, preferably by police. Efforts focus on traffic safety and nuisance, not minor matters like putting out the gray bin one day too early.

117242.explanation: Prevention and safety are important; nuisance spots can therefore be equipped with camera surveillance where necessary.

117247.explanation: Holiday homes provide (temporary) extra housing without extra construction pressure on the Kapelle housing market.

117250.explanation: Help with debt relief is temporary and not automatic. In certain situations this may be necessary, but it should be a temporary measure.

117251.explanation: Municipal help is only for those who really need it. We do not support redistributing residents' wealth through municipal schemes.

117253.explanation: Plans for gradual expansion if needed have been established; options exist to designate extra parking spaces. The market decides if there is demand.

117254.explanation: Through roads may have a 50 KM/h limit, surrounded by a dense network of safe residential streets where 30 km/h is the norm. Roads should be designed accordingly.

117256.explanation: Good healthcare is our priority; this is more important than the lowest price. At the same time, we remain critical of situations where costs may overshadow quality.

117257.explanation: Financial stability is paramount. We advocate for a maximum increase in municipal taxes that aligns with inflation.

117261.explanation: Entrepreneurs may decide on which days they open, just as residents may decide on which days they make use of the offerings.

117264.explanation: We do not support unfair competition with food production.

117268.explanation: Coffeeshops and the problems that can come with them do not fit the character of our villages.

117271.explanation: The solution to the housing shortage is not to have many people living in one home with high rents and the risk of nuisance. We prefer to focus on tiny houses and starter homes.

117272.explanation: Kapelle is not a city, and it should not be treated as one.

117274.explanation: A good location for new construction of special schools must be found as soon as possible, with enough space for the children and good accessibility for the shuttle buses.

117275.explanation: Most residents already go to Goes and Kruiningen; the recycling center in Kapelle is only becoming more limited, making it more efficient to discontinue it altogether. We would have preferred a fully-fledged recycling center in Kapelle, but this proved financially unfeasible.

117219.explanation: At the moment there are sufficient opportunities for residents to express their opinions, such as through the citizen panel Kapelle Participates.

117220.explanation: That is a good idea. Within Kapelle, it would probably be more about a public garden than a sports hall.

117222.explanation: Not always; let's carefully consider this from a positive attitude.

117223.explanation: Such initiatives should be encouraged and supported with appropriate funding.

117224.explanation: No large blocky building near Kapelle, only if we have a very suitable location and it fits the scale of Kapelle.

117225.explanation: As SGP, we are open to sheltering refugees, fellow human beings in need. But it must be appropriate to the size of our small municipality.

117226.explanation: We consider good and responsible housing more important. Employers can also collaborate on this.

117230.explanation: We do not see this as the municipality's task. It can also be a community initiative. It is great if it fosters unity and helps reduce nuisance during New Year's Eve.

117231.explanation: The current system works well and also helps reduce the amount of waste.

117234.explanation: Our countryside must remain as open as possible! We must carefully consider where to build new residential areas. Many interests are involved.

117236.explanation: The municipality must comply with the legal obligation, but no more than that is necessary.

117238.explanation: Beautiful music benefits people. Subsidies for music lessons go just a step too far for us.

117239.explanation: Sports clubs receive sufficient support through subsidies and/or other facilities to maintain their independence.

117241.explanation: The current capacity of BOA (special enforcement officers) is sufficient.

117242.explanation: Camera surveillance should remain an exception and not the rule. Only temporary, when truly necessary.

117247.explanation: This requires a good discussion and weighing of pros and cons.

117250.explanation: Certainly not. We want to help people with the lowest incomes in this way so they do not have to live in poverty.

117251.explanation: We already apply very broad criteria, much broader than the national standards.

117253.explanation: At present there are sufficient charging points; if more are needed, residents can request a charging point.

117254.explanation: We are satisfied with the 30 km/h limit. Keep it as it is now.

117255.explanation: It benefits care if caregiver and client understand each other.

117257.explanation: Due to various national uncertainties regarding finances, it is currently not possible to lower the property tax (OZB).

117261.explanation: Sunday is a God-given day of rest that we need more than ever in this busy society.

117264.explanation: Agricultural land is too valuable to sacrifice for solar panels, especially since there are still plenty of roofs without solar panels.

117268.explanation: The SGP supports a zero growth policy for coffeeshops.

117271.explanation: The housing shortage requires appropriate solutions. Let's carefully consider the pros and cons.

117274.explanation: The Special Schools Kapelle deserve a new location in or outside the center of Kapelle in a suitable place.

117275.explanation: A good balance must be made between costs and benefits before a position can be taken on this.

117219.explanation: The CDA stands for strong representation through the municipal council. Residents must be well involved in important decisions, but responsibility lies with elected officials. Referendums can be advisory but not decisive.

117220.explanation: The CDA believes it is important that residents can actively participate in their neighborhood. They can initiate projects for public green spaces, but for sports halls, we believe the municipality should remain responsible for management and quality. Cooperation comes first, not complete takeover.

117222.explanation: The CDA believes that farmers can develop small-scale extra activities, such as a shop or campsite. However, this is only allowed under conditions and with respect for the surroundings, not automatically or without limits.

117223.explanation: The CDA believes that residents who generate sustainable energy together should primarily receive help and support from the municipality, such as advice and guidance. Financial support is less important than practical assistance to get projects off the ground.

117224.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should critically assess new distribution centers. Establishment should not be automatic but only if it fits with the living environment, employment, and sustainability, so residents and nature are not unnecessarily burdened.

117225.explanation: The CDA believes a large asylum seekers' center is undesirable in our municipality. At the same time, we must comply with the national distribution law. We strive for good (emergency) reception and cooperation, but without unnecessarily burdening the local community.

117226.explanation: The CDA believes that employers in agriculture must provide humane housing for seasonal workers themselves. This ensures that enough homes remain available for our own residents and that housing is close to work. Housing must fit within the surroundings.

117230.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality can offer an alternative to consumer fireworks, such as a professional fireworks show. This ensures a festive end to the year, safe and responsible for people, animals, and the environment.

117231.explanation: The CDA believes bulky waste should be collected free of charge from homes. Collecting it a few times a year is fine; otherwise, residents can take it themselves to the recycling center. This keeps it fair and affordable for everyone.

117234.explanation: The CDA believes farmers may build homes on their land under certain conditions. This is only allowed if it aligns with existing buildings and the landscape is respected so that the living environment of the village is preserved.

117236.explanation: The CDA believes the local broadcaster is important for information and connection in the municipality. Additional support from the municipality is acceptable when needed so the broadcaster can continue its work well for all residents.

117238.explanation: The CDA believes it is important that every primary school child can receive music lessons. With support from the municipality, music becomes accessible to everyone, allowing children to discover and develop their talents.

117239.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality can support sports clubs if they are struggling but still have a future. This keeps clubs alive, allows them to serve their members, and contributes to a healthy and active society.

117241.explanation: The CDA believes more municipal enforcement officers (boa's) are needed for a safe municipality. They provide supervision, enforce rules, and ensure a safe living environment for all residents.

117242.explanation: The CDA believes surveillance cameras should only be used when truly necessary. Their focus must be on safety and livability without unnecessary infringement on residents’ privacy.

117247.explanation: The CDA believes holiday homes may not be used as permanent residences. They are intended for temporary stays and recreation. Permanent living in these homes can lead to problems with infrastructure, amenities, and livability in the neighborhood.

117250.explanation: The CDA believes it is important that residents with low incomes can sometimes have municipal taxes waived. This prevents additional financial problems and helps people make ends meet without unnecessary pressure.

117251.explanation: The CDA believes the income threshold for poverty assistance can be raised. This allows more residents who are struggling to access support so they can participate in society and avoid financial difficulties. The threshold should rise with inflation.

117253.explanation: The CDA believes there are sufficient charging points for electric cars in Kapelle. However, it must remain possible for residents to request a spot so that everyone who really needs one can use a charging point nearby.

117254.explanation: The CDA believes the maximum speed limit within built-up areas should remain at 30 km/h as a standard. This is already the case in Kapelle and contributes to traffic safety, a calm living environment, and more safety for pedestrians and cyclists.

117255.explanation: The CDA believes that people who need care should have the freedom to choose a care provider that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs. This keeps care personal, respectful, and appropriate to everyone’s background. All this, however, on equal cost and quality grounds.

117256.explanation: The CDA believes quality must come first in youth care. Price matters because it is a large cost item, but it should never come at the expense of good care for children and young people who need it.

117257.explanation: The CDA believes property tax (ozb) must be fair. Reduction is not always necessary, but residents must not be unnecessarily burdened. Costs and quality of municipal services must remain well balanced.

117261.explanation: Basically, as CDA, we are not in favor of Sunday shopping days. Existing policy is that store owners can decide for themselves, so economic opportunities are utilized while employees and residents keep their rest days. We see no reason to change the current policy.

117264.explanation: The CDA believes solar panels should not simply be placed on agricultural land. Agricultural land is important for food production and the landscape. Solar panels can only be placed where they do not interfere with agriculture or nature.

117268.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality does not have to allow a coffeeshop. It prevents problems with nuisance and health and fits a policy that protects safety and livability in villages and neighborhoods.

117271.explanation: The CDA believes landlords may rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses. This offers flexible housing, helps starters and students, and contributes to more efficient use of the existing housing stock.

117272.explanation: The CDA believes Kapelle’s center should not become completely car-free. Cars remain important for accessibility to shops and amenities, but roads can be made safe and clear for everyone.

117274.explanation: The CDA believes that when building new Special Schools, their location must be considered. Moving out of the center is possible, provided that education, accessibility, and the living environment remain optimal.

117275.explanation: The CDA believes there must remain a recycling center in Kapelle. This allows residents to easily and nearby dispose of waste, encourages reuse and recycling, and keeps the municipality clean and livable.

117219.explanation: Residents should be able to participate as much as possible in topics that affect them.

117220.explanation: Residents may be held responsible for keeping public spaces clean and not littering carelessly. However, maintenance of public facilities is the responsibility of the municipality.

117222.explanation: It is important that the municipality keeps track of the number of farm campsites and shops. Shops are important for preserving the municipality’s central function.

117223.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that sustainable energy is generated as much as possible by residents themselves. Financial support can encourage this.

117224.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds this statement too general. If the environment and nature are too severely impacted, GroenLinks-PvdA believes the establishment of a distribution center should be opposed.

117225.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality must comply with its legal obligation under the distribution law and accommodate 80 people currently in an asylum procedure.

117226.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, it is important that seasonal workers are housed on their own company sites. The housing must meet strict regulations.

117231.explanation: Residents of the municipality of Kapelle can bring their bulky waste to the recycling center.

117234.explanation: Their land has an agricultural designation and no houses can be built on it. It is up to the municipal council to determine the designation of a specific area.

117236.explanation: A local broadcaster is important for local democracy.

117238.explanation: Making music is important for the development of children. Students from the municipality of Kapelle can currently attend the Zeeland Music School for music lessons, to which the municipality contributes.

117239.explanation: Playing sports is important for health. Associations are important for social connections within our municipality. They should be supported as much as possible when they are facing difficulties.

117241.explanation: To relieve the neighborhood police officers, more enforcement officers (boa's) need to be deployed.

117242.explanation: There are several locations in the municipality where a lot of hidden crime takes place and where additional camera surveillance is necessary.

117251.explanation: Providing help to multiple people prevents more complex problems in the end, such as debt issues.

117254.explanation: Driving at 30 km/h is important for traffic safety in the municipality of Kapelle.

117256.explanation: The municipality must primarily focus on the quality of youth care. Cheap solutions are ultimately expensive.

117257.explanation: Property tax may only be increased by the inflation rate.

117271.explanation: The housing problem can only be solved by building more homes.

117274.explanation: A suitable location must first be found for this school.

117219.explanation: It must be clear in advance that this opinion does not necessarily represent the majority view of the council (or the executive board). So, no binding referendum.

117220.explanation: That should be possible with clear agreements.

117222.explanation: That may not always be possible due to spatial planning rules. Custom solutions are required.

117224.explanation: Kapelle is not the center of the Bevelanden, Zeeland, or the Southwestern Delta. Those distribution centers may be built at other locations.

117225.explanation: Christian Union proposes to make long-term agreements with the COA about a (multi-target group) asylum seeker center.

117226.explanation: With sufficient municipal supervision of financial and social exploitation.

117230.explanation: We can perfectly celebrate New Year's Eve without fireworks. If it still ‘must’ happen, both private individuals and businesses should also contribute for financial balance in these expenses. Not a breaking point for the Christian Union.

117231.explanation: Principle: 'the polluter pays' is a responsible position. 'Free' does not exist; someone ultimately has to pay for it.

117234.explanation: Provided that it fits within the spatial planning agreements.

117236.explanation: There are already agreements about that. If extra money is needed, the local broadcaster can raise the alarm.

117238.explanation: The right to music education is also a task for municipalities. The municipal contribution primarily serves the broader goal of raising every child to be ‘musical’. Subsidies only based on minimum income schemes.

117239.explanation: That depends on the situation; the efforts of sports clubs and their volunteers themselves should be paramount.

117241.explanation: There is a pool of enforcement officers (BOA’s) at De Bevelanden, deployed across the different municipalities as needed. We have not heard any calls for an increase in their number.

117242.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used in extreme cases. It is, by the way, the responsibility of the mayor; the municipal council does not decide on this.

117247.explanation: It is undesirable to allow that. In the past, this caused conflicts between recreational, migrant, and permanent residents. It is also unclear to enforce.

117251.explanation: There are various schemes that are revised annually or regularly. Different percentages are applied in practice for different minimum income schemes.

117253.explanation: This is being addressed regionally in collaboration with the provinces of South Holland and Zeeland and about 40 other municipalities. The rollout runs until mid-2030.

117254.explanation: Attention should mainly be paid to the road design of 30-km/h roads. Furthermore, the Vroonlandseweg should partly still be executed as a bicycle street.

117256.explanation: And definitely also on the quality of care.

117261.explanation: We do not need to find a solution for a non-existent problem. Moreover, it is better for people to have a day of rest every week; that biblical example deserves to be followed!

117264.explanation: In principle, no. Agricultural land: it’s in the name.

117268.explanation: Drugs are bad for a person; therefore, the current APV (General Local Regulation) on this point should not be changed.

117271.explanation: Provided the landlord also continues to live there (hospitalization).

117272.explanation: Accessibility of shops and facilities must be guaranteed for every mobility issue.

117274.explanation: Given the amount of vehicle movements, that would indeed be advisable.

117275.explanation: That is desirable for a number of waste streams. By the way, a complete recycling center where all waste streams can be delivered in Goes or Kruiningen is close enough.

117219.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe that residents should have a say in important decisions. A referendum can strengthen local democracy, provided it is carefully designed and complements the role of the council.

117220.explanation: It is better for the cohesion in a neighborhood. And if people can do something themselves without interference from the municipality, it can also save costs.

117222.explanation: D66 supports fewer restrictive rules where possible. And it is also good if farmers can do business in a flexible way.

117223.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we consider local energy initiatives important. A loan should definitely be possible, but a grant in the form of a subsidy may not be necessary, since sustainable energy generally already saves costs.

117224.explanation: It depends on various aspects that also apply to other company locations. Such as the extent to which they contribute to local employment, fit into the environment, and sustainability.

117225.explanation: D66 supports a proportional contribution to asylum reception as set out in the distribution law. Furthermore, D66 supports research into structural reception as this provides more stability in the long term, enables more humane care, and is cheaper than temporary emergency shelter.

117226.explanation: It should not be required but it is certainly allowed. The most important thing is that the reception takes place decently and that there are good controls.

117230.explanation: Given the private fireworks ban, it is good for the municipality to organize a joint event with residents that includes festivities and possibly fireworks.

117231.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe bulky waste should be collected accessibly, but free home collection for everyone is not necessary. Targeted customization (e.g., for less mobile residents) and good waste policy are more important.

117234.explanation: Given the housing shortage, we must tackle all possibilities to create more living space. Clear guidelines must be established and it must be feasible considering factors like energy supply, drainage, and infrastructure.

117236.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe a strong local broadcaster is important for local democracy and information provision. But especially considering the difficult financial years ahead, extra funds are not needed; current funding is sufficient.

117238.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe every child deserves equal opportunities. Music education stimulates creativity and development. With subsidies, we ensure that children from lower-income families can also participate.

117239.explanation: D66 Kapelle believes sports clubs are essential for health and social cohesion. If they get into trouble, the municipality may temporarily provide financial support, with clear conditions.

117241.explanation: At certain times, such as in spring/summer on the beach at Wemeldinge, it would be good to have extra supervision to prevent nuisance, but more community officers is not an end in itself. We should examine whether better cooperation, prevention, or targeted deployment might be more effective.

117242.explanation: Structural camera surveillance has too much impact on people's personal privacy and should only be applied in the most necessary situations.

117247.explanation: Flexible use of living space can help address the housing shortage and can certainly play a role here.

117250.explanation: It is humane and fair that debt cancellation takes place when people truly cannot pay, and to ensure these people do not get into further financial trouble or deeper debt.

117251.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe the income limit is basically sufficient, but implementation must improve: more accessible, less bureaucracy, and more actively reaching people who are currently overlooked.

117253.explanation: More public charging points are needed to enable electric driving for everyone. This supports the energy transition, prevents charging stress, and ensures people without their own driveway can charge as well.

117254.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we say: 30 km/h is the norm in most places, but not everywhere. On thoroughfare roads, 50 km/h can logically remain.

117255.explanation: As D66, we believe care should never be refused based on religion, culture or belief, although customization should be offered where possible. Saying “no” too often undermines equal access to care.

117256.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe quality and thus price-quality ratio are decisive in youth care. Too much focus on price harms appropriate support and can lead to bigger problems and higher costs in the long run.

117257.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we believe the property tax (OZB) must remain affordable, but it is also necessary for facilities, especially in the coming years when we will receive less money from the national government.

117261.explanation: As D66 Kapelle, we stand for freedom of choice. Entrepreneurs and residents should decide for themselves whether shops are open. The municipality should not prohibit this but allow room for local considerations.

117264.explanation: Solar panels, including on land, are important for the energy transition. Moreover, the farmer must also be free to decide if it is necessary to make ends meet. Good rules must be established for local participation, increasing biodiversity, and national integration.

117268.explanation: As far as D66 Kapelle is concerned, this should be possible; a coffee shop is not illegal. Attention must be paid to public health and safety, just as with, for example, alcohol. Especially concerning our youth.

117271.explanation: D66 Kapelle has emphasized this regularly in recent years; this is a good solution to make vacant space in existing homes inhabited. It can also help socially, for example through the hospita arrangement where someone lives with an elderly person and can assist as needed.

117272.explanation: It is necessary for supermarkets to be accessible by car, but it would also be good to design the center more for pedestrians and cyclists. D66 wants more space and liveliness for pedestrians and cyclists, for instance by making Kerkplein low-traffic or car-free.

117274.explanation: D66 believes the current location is inadequate and that a new single location should be sought. This can be either inside or outside the center.

117275.explanation: It should be made as easy as possible to properly sort or reuse waste close by. A recycling center, even if limited, in Kapelle can help. D66 believes additional opportunities for waste separation should exist at the village or neighborhood level.

statements.title: There should continue to be a recycling center in Kapelle.

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The residents of Kapelle should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Kapelle moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Kapelle should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
There should be more camera surveillance in Kapelle.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The center of Kapelle should become car-free.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The Special Schools should relocate from the center of Kapelle.
